settingsTour.appearance.title: Appearance
settingsTour.appearance.desc: Visual theme and interface language. Changes apply immediately.
settingsTour.status.title: Status
settingsTour.status.desc: Runtime state of the backend and your configured model services — always visible here on the hub.
settingsTour.network.title: Network
settingsTour.network.desc: Ports, the browser-facing API base, and CORS origins — for Docker, LAN, and reverse-proxy deployments.
settingsTour.models.title: Models
settingsTour.models.desc: Open this to configure language, embedding, search, voice, and generation models — all seven services live here.
settingsTour.knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
settingsTour.knowledge.desc: Choose the document parsing engine that turns uploads into text.
settingsTour.chat.title: Chat
settingsTour.chat.desc: Tools, MCP servers, and per-capability runtime knobs.
settingsTour.llm.title: LLM
settingsTour.llm.desc: Manage LLM profiles — provider, endpoint, and model. The active model powers chat and most agent reasoning.
settingsTour.embedding.title: Embedding
settingsTour.embedding.desc: Configure the embedding provider and dimensions used for retrieval and knowledge-base ingestion.
settingsTour.search.title: Search
settingsTour.search.desc: Pick the web-search provider and tune proxy / request settings.
settingsTour.capabilities.title: Capabilities
settingsTour.capabilities.desc: Tune per-capability LLM params and runtime caps for every capability surfaced in chat (chat / solve / question / research / …).
settingsTour.memory.title: Memory
settingsTour.memory.desc: Tune the memory consolidator: chunking, LLM budgets, dedup, and reference policies.
settingsTour.mcp.title: MCP servers
settingsTour.mcp.desc: Connect external MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to expose more tools to the agent (coming soon).
settingsTour.tools.title: settingsTour.tools.title
settingsTour.apply.title: Save & Apply
settingsTour.apply.desc: Save Draft persists your changes; Apply activates them right away. Both controls live in the top toolbar, always within reach.
settings.configNote: Save Draft persists all profiles to model_catalog.json, which DeepTutor reads at runtime. Apply activates the selected profile and refreshes runtime clients. You can keep multiple profiles in the catalog and switch between them anytime.

The active embedding configuration doesn: The active embedding configuration doesn't match any ready index version. Re-index to rebuild against the current embedding model.
Previous indexing failed. Retry will reb: Previous indexing failed. Retry will rebuild the index from the existing source documents.
No files yet. Add one using the right pa: No files yet. Add one using the right panel.
Drop files here to add them to this know: Drop files here to add them to this knowledge base. New files are indexed against the active embedding model.
This knowledge base is in Error state. R: This knowledge base is in Error state. Retry indexing from the existing documents before uploading new files.
Stale (matches active config but storage: Stale (matches active config but storage is empty)

Switch user-toggleable tools on or off. Locked tools are mounted automatically when the chat agent needs them.
Auto-mounted by the agent when needed. N: Auto-mounted by the agent when needed. Not user-toggleable.
Tune the app, configure model services,: Tune the app, configure model services, and inspect built-in tools.
Tune the visual theme and interface lang: Tune the visual theme and interface language. Changes apply immediately and are stored in your account.
Pick the colour palette you prefer.
Pick the colour palette and interface st: Pick the colour palette and interface style. Each tile previews the theme it applies.
Snow is the warm default; Light is a coo: Snow is the warm default; Light is a cool blue-grey; Dark mirrors Snow's accent on a warm near-black; Glass adds translucent panels on pure black.
Cream is a warm, paper-like default with: Cream is a warm, paper-like default with a terracotta accent. Snow is its cool, blue-tinted sibling with a royal-blue accent. Dark keeps Cream's warmth on near-black. Glass adds translucent purple panels on a deep gradient.
Snow keeps things minimal; Glass adds tr: Snow keeps things minimal; Glass adds translucent panels. Dark mode follows the same accent palette.
Affects the UI only. Model output langua: Affects the UI only. Model output language is controlled by your prompt.
Runtime status for backend and configure: Runtime status for backend and configured services.
Live state of the backend and the model: Live state of the backend and the model services your configuration applies to.
Status reflects the runtime values after: Status reflects the runtime values after the last Apply. Draft changes only take effect once applied.
Ports, browser API base, and CORS origin: Ports, browser API base, and CORS origins.
Control the browser-facing API URL and C: Control the browser-facing API URL and CORS origins used by Docker, LAN, and reverse-proxy deployments.
Network settings saved. Restart DeepTuto: Network settings saved. Restart DeepTutor to apply them.
These ports are read during startup. Doc: These ports are read during startup. Docker port mappings must match the container-side values.
Next.js serves the Web UI on this port.
Set this when the browser cannot reach t: Set this when the browser cannot reach the backend through localhost, such as remote Docker or a reverse proxy.
Leave blank for local Docker. Use the ex: Leave blank for local Docker. Use the externally reachable backend URL, including any proxy path.
Only required for authenticated cross-or: Only required for authenticated cross-origin deployments. Use frontend origins, not API URLs.
Comma, semicolon, and newline separators: Comma, semicolon, and newline separators are accepted. Bare host:port values are stored as http://host:port.
Auth is enabled but secure cookies are o: Auth is enabled but secure cookies are off. Cross-site HTTPS deployments should set auth.cookie_secure=true and restart so SameSite=None cookies work in modern browsers.
Network changes are written to data/user: Network changes are written to data/user/settings/system.json and take effect after restart.
Built-in tools the chat agent can invoke: Built-in tools the chat agent can invoke.
Built-in tools the chat agent can invoke. Each shows its description, parameters, and prompt hints in your current language.
Connect external MCP (Model Context Prot: Connect external MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to extend the agent's capabilities.
MCP server support is coming soon: MCP server support is coming soon
We're working on a way to register and a: We're working on a way to register and authenticate MCP servers from this page so the chat agent can call tools they expose. Until then, this section is reserved as a placeholder.
Language model providers and active prof: Language model providers and active profile.
Configure language model profiles. The a: Configure language model profiles. The active model is used for chat and most agent reasoning.
Configure embedding model profiles. Used: Configure embedding model profiles. Used by retrieval and knowledge-base ingestion.
Configure web search providers. Used by: Configure web search providers. Used by the web_search tool and any agent step that hits the open web.
Memory tooltip: Long-term memory the tutor keeps across sessions.

Every L1 trace event, click to jump to t: Every L1 trace event, click to jump to the source.
{{n}} items in workspace not yet in trac: {{n}} items in workspace not yet in trace
L1 mirrors your workspace, L2 summarises: L1 mirrors your workspace, L2 summarises per-surface content, L3 is cross-surface knowledge.
Live snapshot of your workspace — one en: Live snapshot of your workspace — one entry per real artifact.
Re-scan workspace and record any changes: Re-scan workspace and record any changes
No changes recorded yet. Run Refresh to: No changes recorded yet. Run Refresh to capture the baseline.
Workspace changed since last refresh. Cl: Workspace changed since last refresh. Click Refresh to commit these to the changes log.
Pending — not yet committed to changes l: Pending — not yet committed to changes log
Empty. Click Update to consolidate from: Empty. Click Update to consolidate from the current snapshot.
Per-surface summaries consolidated from: Per-surface summaries consolidated from L1 content.
Everything DeepTutor remembers about you: Everything DeepTutor remembers about you, organised across three layers. Click into any layer to inspect or curate it.
Snapshot of your live workspace across {: Snapshot of your live workspace across {{n}} surfaces. Refresh to record changes.
Surface-specific facts extracted by the: Surface-specific facts extracted by the consolidator. Run Update / Audit / Dedup per doc.
Cross-surface synthesis: profile, recent: Cross-surface synthesis: profile, recent timeline, knowledge scope. Hedged claims with L2 evidence.
See all three layers at once — L3 synthe: See all three layers at once — L3 synthesis at the centre, L2 facts in the middle, L1 traces on the outside. Hover any node for a preview.
Pick a surface to open its workbench. Ea: Pick a surface to open its workbench. Each L2 doc has Update / Audit / Dedup modes.
Cross-surface synthesis: profile, recent: Cross-surface synthesis: profile, recent timeline, knowledge scope. Each slot has its own workbench.
A rolling timeline across all surfaces,: A rolling timeline across all surfaces, last 1–4 weeks.
Durable identity, learning style, knowle: Durable identity, learning style, knowledge level — only multi-surface evidence.
Concepts you've engaged with, tagged fam: Concepts you've engaged with, tagged familiar / practicing / unsure.
Surface-specific facts about the user. U: Surface-specific facts about the user. Update extracts new facts; Audit checks vs raw evidence; Dedup tidies.
Cross-surface synthesis. Hedged claims w: Cross-surface synthesis. Hedged claims with L2 evidence.
Empty. Click Update to extract facts fro: Empty. Click Update to extract facts from your traces.
Extract facts from new traces since last: Extract facts from new traces since last update. Append-only.
Check existing facts against full raw ev: Check existing facts against full raw evidence. Line-level edits.
Iteratively merge duplicates & rewrite m: Iteratively merge duplicates & rewrite muddled entries.
Tune the chunk-based consolidator: how m: Tune the chunk-based consolidator: how many LLM rounds per Update / Audit / Dedup, how aggressively to chunk, and how strictly to validate references.
Default LLM rounds for chunk-based incre: Default LLM rounds for chunk-based incremental fact extraction. Per-doc overrides live in the workbench.
Maximum chunks the chunker emits for an: Maximum chunks the chunker emits for an L2 update.
Maximum chunks for an L3 update across t: Maximum chunks for an L3 update across the 7 L2 docs.
Default LLM rounds for line-level edits: Default LLM rounds for line-level edits against raw evidence.
Iterative dedup over the full doc. Stops: Iterative dedup over the full doc. Stops early when an iteration emits zero edits.
Lower-level knobs that shape how content: Lower-level knobs that shape how content is split.
Fraction of chunk size carried into the: Fraction of chunk size carried into the next chunk. 0–0.5.
Where the chunker prefers to cut.
How strictly facts must cite the chunk t: How strictly facts must cite the chunk they were extracted from.
Drops facts that come back without a ref: Drops facts that come back without a ref.
Drop invalid refs (vs reject the whole f: Drop invalid refs (vs reject the whole fact)
On: keep the fact, drop only out-of-pool: On: keep the fact, drop only out-of-pool refs. Off: any bad ref rejects the fact.
Did not receive a final edit result.
The draft changed before AI edit finishe: The draft changed before AI edit finished. Please reselect the text and try again.
Backend returned an unexpected payload a: Backend returned an unexpected payload after save.
Failed to load capability settings (HTTP: Failed to load capability settings (HTTP {{status}}). Make sure the backend is running the latest build — the /api/v1/capabilities/settings endpoint was added in this release.
The backend returned an unexpected paylo: The backend returned an unexpected payload for /api/v1/capabilities/settings. Restart the backend to pick up the latest schema.
Budget for the user-forced short-circuit: Budget for the user-forced short-circuit response.
Cap on the explore loop before planning.
Deep research pipeline. Iteration count: Deep research pipeline. Iteration count is controlled per request via the depth selector (quick / standard / deep / manual) in the chat input, not here.
Deep solve runs as one agent loop with a: Deep solve runs as one agent loop with a plan / finish-step / replan spine.
Hard cap on chat loop iterations per tur: Hard cap on chat loop iterations per turn.
How far back paper search will scan.
How many times the planner may revise th: How many times the planner may revise the plan within one turn.
Total LLM-round budget for one solve tur: Total LLM-round budget for one solve turn (plan, tool calls and finishing all count).
Per-capability LLM parameters and runtim: Per-capability LLM parameters and runtime knobs. Values are persisted to data/user/settings/agents.yaml (LLM params) and data/user/settings/main.yaml (runtime knobs).
Per-iteration budget for the chat LLM ca: Per-iteration budget for the chat LLM call (which emits THINK / TOOL / FINISH labels in a single stream).
Per-tool wall-clock timeout for the rese: Per-tool wall-clock timeout for the researching phase.
Sampling temperature shared across every: Sampling temperature shared across every chat stage.
Summarize long tool outputs to keep the: Summarize long tool outputs to keep the prompt budget low.
The agentic chat loop. Stage budgets cap: The agentic chat loop. Stage budgets cap max_tokens per stage; lower the intermediate stages on small-context models.
Pick a mode and click Run. The LLM trace: Pick a mode and click Run. The LLM trace — system prompt, user prompt, response — appears here, turn by turn.
Reset will delete the current memory fil: Reset will delete the current memory file AND its seen-id state. The next Update will re-ingest every L1 entity from scratch. Continue?
Pick an option or type your own to conti: Pick an option or type your own to continue.
Unanswered questions will be submitted a: Unanswered questions will be submitted as skipped.
{{count}} questions — tap a tab to switc: {{count}} questions — tap a tab to switch.
Many sites refuse to embed for security: Many sites refuse to embed for security reasons. If the page below stays blank, use “Open in browser”.
Paste a URL or open a local file. Attach: Paste a URL or open a local file. Attachments from chat will also land here.
This site looks like it refused to embed: This site looks like it refused to embed (its security headers block iframes). Use “Open in browser” to view it in a real tab.
Pick a document to view or update: Pick a document to view or update
Preferences are written when you explici: Preferences are written when you explicitly tell the chat assistant your preferences (style, language, format).
Preferences is written by the chat assis: Preferences is written by the chat assistant, not consolidated.
Stored at memory/backup/{{name}}. v2 sta: Stored at memory/backup/{{name}}. v2 starts fresh — interact with DeepTutor and click Update on each doc to build memory.
As you chat, the tools, references and a: As you chat, the tools, references and attachments you use will appear here.
No-LLM consolidation: collapse duplicate: No-LLM consolidation: collapse duplicate ref rows into one footnote each, then renumber. Idempotent, safe to run any time.
Click to open an interactive GeoGebra ca: Click to open an interactive GeoGebra canvas in the side viewer.
GeoGebra viewer is not available in this: GeoGebra viewer is not available in this surface
Embedding requests are sent to this URL: Embedding requests are sent to this URL exactly; DeepTutor does not append /embeddings or /api/embed at request time.
Model endpoints are assigned by your adm: Model endpoints are assigned by your administrator. You can still personalize theme and language here.
Backend unreachable — model endpoints wi: Backend unreachable — model endpoints will appear once the connection is restored. See the banner above for details.
Browse, rename, delete, and reopen previ: Browse, rename, delete, and reopen previous conversations from your learning space.
Could not load settings from the backend: Could not load settings from the backend.
Verify the backend is running and NEXT_P: Verify the backend is running and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE points to a reachable host. For Docker, see data/user/settings/system.json.
Describe the chart, diagram, or animatio: Describe the chart, diagram, or animation you want to visualize...
L3 synthesis at the centre, L2 facts in: L3 synthesis at the centre, L2 facts in the middle ring, L1 traces on the outside.
Click to lock · open in workbench: Click to lock · open in workbench
Hover a node to preview the memory. Clic: Hover a node to preview the memory. Click to lock the highlight and trace its references inward (L1 → L2 → L3) or outward.
PDF parsing backend (local or cloud API): PDF parsing backend (local or cloud API).
PDF parsing backend used when generating: PDF parsing backend used when generating questions from an uploaded exam paper. Run MinerU locally, or use the hosted mineru.net cloud API.
Documents are uploaded to mineru.net for: Documents are uploaded to mineru.net for parsing.
Parsing runs on this machine using the l: Parsing runs on this machine using the local MinerU install.
Local mode requires the MinerU CLI on th: Local mode requires the MinerU CLI on the server (pip install mineru). If it is not installed, switch to Cloud API mode.
Get an API token from mineru.net → API m: Get an API token from mineru.net → API management.
Override only if you use a self-hosted M: Override only if you use a self-hosted MinerU endpoint.
A token is saved. Type to replace it; le: A token is saved. Type to replace it; leave blank to keep it.
Verifies the token against the MinerU AP: Verifies the token against the MinerU API (no quota used).
Forwarded to the MinerU API for each doc: Forwarded to the MinerU API for each document.
pipeline is faster; vlm is the vision-la: pipeline is faster; vlm is the vision-language model.
Document language hint. Use \"auto\" to: Document language hint. Use "auto" to let MinerU detect it.
Treat the PDF as scanned images. Slower;: Treat the PDF as scanned images. Slower; only for non-text PDFs.
MinerU settings are written to data/user: MinerU settings are written to data/user/settings/document_parsing.json.
The MinerU CLI must be visible to the De: The MinerU CLI must be visible to the DeepTutor server process.
MinerU CLI not found on PATH.
Install into the server environment: uv: Install into the server environment: uv pip install -U "mineru[core]" — first parse downloads ~1-2 GB of models.
The MinerU CLI must be reachable by the: The MinerU CLI must be reachable by the DeepTutor server process. Reserve >=20 GB of free disk (official guidance): ~2-4 GB install, ~1-2 GB models lazily downloaded on first parse, plus parsing cache.
Configured CLI path is not executable.
Install into any Python environment: uv: Install into any Python environment: uv pip install -U "mineru[core]" — then set the CLI path below, or install into the server environment for PATH auto-detection.
Optional. Point to a mineru executable i: Optional. Point to a mineru executable in an isolated environment (uv tool, pipx, conda) to avoid dependency conflicts. Leave blank to auto-detect from PATH.
Local parsing needs ~1-2 GB of model wei: Local parsing needs ~1-2 GB of model weights. They download lazily on the first parse, or download them ahead of time here.
ModelScope is usually faster in mainland: ModelScope is usually faster in mainland China.
Custom HuggingFace endpoint or mirror (e: Custom HuggingFace endpoint or mirror (e.g. a regional mirror). Leave blank for the official address.
Runs mineru-models-download on the serve: Runs mineru-models-download on the server using the source and address above.
Companions with their own soul, library,: Companions with their own soul, library, and channels — reachable from your IM apps.
Create a partner, give it a soul and a s: Create a partner, give it a soul and a slice of your library, then talk to it here or from Feishu, Telegram, Slack and more.
A companion that lives on your channels: A companion that lives on your channels and works from its own library.
Who this partner is. Start from a templa: Who this partner is. Start from a template, clone a chat persona, or write it yourself.
The model it thinks with and the tools i: The model it thinks with and the tools it may use.
Give it a slice of your knowledge — copi: Give it a slice of your knowledge — copied into the partner's own workspace.
You can connect Feishu, Telegram, Slack: You can connect Feishu, Telegram, Slack and more right after creating.
No personas in your chat workspace yet.
The persona's markdown is copied into th: The persona's markdown is copied into the partner — later edits to the persona won't affect it.

Selected items are copied into the partn: Selected items are copied into the partner's private workspace.
Say hello — this conversation shares the: Say hello — this conversation shares the same memory your partner has on its connected channels.
Partner is stopped. Start it before chat: Partner is stopped. Start it before chatting.
Delete this partner and ALL its data (wo: Delete this partner and ALL its data (workspace, sessions, channels)? This cannot be undone.
Model updated — applies from the next me: Model updated — applies from the next message
Tools updated — applies from the next me: Tools updated — applies from the next message
The partner's persona — injected into ev: The partner's persona — injected into every conversation.
Applies to this partner only; falls back: Applies to this partner only; falls back to the system default.
What this partner is allowed to use.
Knowledge bases, skills, and notebooks c: Knowledge bases, skills, and notebooks copied into this partner's workspace.
Nothing assigned yet — this partner only: Nothing assigned yet — this partner only knows what you tell it.
Config is saved on disk; stop and start: Config is saved on disk; stop and start the partner to apply.
Couldn't render this document — use Down: Couldn't render this document — use Download to open it.
Couldn't render this spreadsheet — use D: Couldn't render this spreadsheet — use Download to open it.
This workbook has no sheets to preview.
Large sheet — preview truncated. Downloa: Large sheet — preview truncated. Download for the full file.
Could not load the model catalog — the p: Could not load the model catalog — the partner will use the system default.
Follows whatever the system default mode: Follows whatever the system default model is.
No models configured yet — add providers: No models configured yet — add providers in Settings → LLM.
A name and a face — everything else can: A name and a face — everything else can change later.
Almost there — check the essentials befo: Almost there — check the essentials before creating.
The primary model answers every turn; if: The primary model answers every turn; if it fails outright, the turn is retried once on the backup.
Add some content before running auto-mar: Add some content before running auto-mark.
The draft changed while AI edit was runn: The draft changed while AI edit was running. Please try again.
This feature is being reworked and will: This feature is being reworked and will be back soon.
Use a PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, or SVG image.
This channel is not available on the ser: This channel is not available on the server.
Edited — your version becomes this partn: Edited — your version becomes this partner's soul. The original template is untouched.
Mastery-based tutoring with a hard gate: Mastery-based tutoring with a hard gate
Mastery Path tooltip: Mastery-based learning with a hard gate
This engine needs an API key. Configure: This engine needs an API key. Configure it before creating.
PageIndex is a hosted, vectorless retrie: PageIndex is a hosted, vectorless retrieval engine. Documents in a PageIndex knowledge base are uploaded to PageIndex's servers for processing. One key is shared by all your PageIndex knowledge bases.
Manage your knowledge bases and retrieva: Manage your knowledge bases and retrieval engines.
Connect your Obsidian vault. The tutor n: Connect your Obsidian vault. The tutor navigates and writes notes in place — no upload, no index. Local / self-hosted only.
Point DeepTutor at a folder that is your: Point DeepTutor at a folder that is your Obsidian vault. Nothing is uploaded or copied — DeepTutor reads (and, when you ask, writes) the notes in place. The path must be reachable by the server, so this works on a local / self-hosted DeepTutor.
The absolute path to the vault folder on: The absolute path to the vault folder on this machine.
Upload documents and build a fresh index: Upload documents and build a fresh index.
Reuse an index you already built — read: Reuse an index you already built — read in place, no upload or re-index.
This engine's index lives in the cloud a: This engine's index lives in the cloud and can't be linked.
A live Obsidian vault — browsed and edit: A live Obsidian vault — browsed and edited in place, no index.
The absolute path to a knowledge base fo: The absolute path to a knowledge base folder on this machine — nothing is copied.
Create one to upload documents and retri: Create one to upload documents and retrieve grounded context in chat.
Unsupported files are skipped; the rest: Unsupported files are skipped; the rest will be indexed.
Describe images with the vision model: Describe images with the vision model
Failed to load document parsing settings: Failed to load document parsing settings.
Failed to save document parsing settings: Failed to save document parsing settings.
Forwarded to MinerU for each document.
How uploaded documents are converted int: How uploaded documents are converted into text for knowledge bases and question generation. Pick an engine and its options. Local model downloads are off by default — they only happen when you explicitly allow them.
Lightweight Markdown conversion with bro: Lightweight Markdown conversion with broad format support. No model downloads.
Local parsing needs ~1-2 GB of model wei: Local parsing needs ~1-2 GB of model weights. By default they are NOT downloaded automatically — download them explicitly below, or allow automatic download on first parse.
Built-in plain text extraction for PDF,: Built-in plain text extraction for PDF, Office, and text files. No optional parser package, model download, OCR, or layout reconstruction.
No knowledge bases use this engine yet.
This engine uses your active chat and em: This engine uses your active chat and embedding models. Manage them in the model catalog.
This engine ships as an optional depende: This engine ships as an optional dependency. Install it, then restart DeepTutor to enable it.
The default for new searches. A knowledg: The default for new searches. A knowledge base can still override it per-KB.
BM25 keyword + vector semantic retrieval: BM25 keyword + vector semantic retrieval, fused and re-ranked. More robust recall.
Vector semantic retrieval only. Faster,: Vector semantic retrieval only. Faster, but leans entirely on embedding quality.
Entity-focused retrieval over the releva: Entity-focused retrieval over the relevant local subgraph — fast and economical.
Map-reduce over global community summari: Map-reduce over global community summaries — best for broad, thematic questions.
Local retrieval with dynamic follow-ups: Local retrieval with dynamic follow-ups — balances precision and coverage.
Plain vector retrieval — the lightest op: Plain vector retrieval — the lightest option.
Plain vector retrieval, without the know: Plain vector retrieval, without the knowledge graph.
Local context focused on the most releva: Local context focused on the most relevant entities.
Combines local and global retrieval — a: Combines local and global retrieval — a solid general default.
Multimodal documents need a vision-capab: Multimodal documents need a vision-capable chat model.
Local vector engine — works out of the b: Local vector engine — works out of the box. Retrieval uses your active embedding model; install the optional BM25 package to enable hybrid retrieval.
Hosted engine: documents are uploaded to: Hosted engine: documents are uploaded to PageIndex's servers for processing. Requires an API key; PDF / Markdown only.
Local knowledge-graph retrieval. Needs t: Local knowledge-graph retrieval. Needs the optional dependency installed; indexing is LLM-heavy. Requires an active chat model and embedding model.
Graph + vector retrieval with multimodal: Graph + vector retrieval with multimodal parsing. Needs the optional dependency installed; indexing is LLM-heavy. Requires active chat and embedding models; multimodal also needs a vision model.
Off by default. When off, a local parse: Off by default. When off, a local parse fails with guidance instead of silently downloading multi-GB weights on first run. Turn on to let the first parse fetch models, or use the explicit download below.
Off by default. When off, parsing fails: Off by default. When off, parsing fails with guidance instead of silently downloading models. Or pre-fetch with `docling-tools models download`.
Reserved — uses DeepTutor's vision model: Reserved — uses DeepTutor's vision model to caption images during conversion.
Structured conversion of PDF/Office/HTML: Structured conversion of PDF/Office/HTML/images. Downloads layout/table models on first run.
The active engine handles all parsing. m: The active engine handles all parsing. markitdown is lightweight and needs no model downloads; MinerU and Docling produce richer structure but download local models (gated below) or use a hosted API.
The active engine handles all parsing. T: The active engine handles all parsing. Text-only is built in and extracts plain text; markitdown is lightweight and optional; MinerU and Docling produce richer structure but may need local models or a hosted API.
Conversations imported from Claude Code: Conversations imported from Claude Code and Codex.
Bring your Claude Code and Codex history: Bring your Claude Code and Codex history into your space, then keep the conversation going.
Pick your local .claude or .codex folder: Pick your local .claude or .codex folder — DeepTutor finds each project and brings the chats in.
This folder has no readable chat history: This folder has no readable chat history. Pick your .claude or .codex folder.
Select your local .claude or .codex fold: Select your local .claude or .codex folder. DeepTutor reads it right here in your browser — nothing leaves your machine until you choose what to import.
It auto-detects the tool, groups convers: It auto-detects the tool, groups conversations by project, and lets you pick exactly what to bring in.
Your browser doesn't support folder acce: Your browser doesn't support folder access. Please use a Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Arc).
Tip: in the folder picker, press ⌘⇧. (Ma: Tip: in the folder picker, press ⌘⇧. (Mac) to reveal hidden folders like .claude.
They're ready in your space — open one t: They're ready in your space — open one to keep chatting.
This folder doesn't look like a .claude: This folder doesn't look like a .claude or .codex home. Please select the right folder.
Your browser doesn't support folder acce: Your browser doesn't support folder access. Please use a Chromium-based browser.
Something went wrong while importing. Pl: Something went wrong while importing. Please try again.
Your Claude Code and Codex sessions, org: Your Claude Code and Codex sessions, organized by source — open one to keep chatting, or refresh to pull in new conversations.
Add your Claude Code or Codex folder to: Add your Claude Code or Codex folder to bring its conversations in — then chat with them or refresh anytime.
Refresh failed — try re-adding the agent: Refresh failed — try re-adding the agent.
Hidden folders like .claude won't show b: Hidden folders like .claude won't show by default — press ⌘⇧. (Command-Shift-Period) in the picker to reveal them.
Bring conversations from your imported a: Bring conversations from your imported agents into this turn.
Search conversations by title or last me: Search conversations by title or last message
Reference imported Claude Code / Codex c: Reference imported Claude Code / Codex conversations.
Pick an agent, then choose the conversat: Pick an agent, then choose the conversations to bring in.
Pick the days to bring in. Refreshing la: Pick the days to bring in. Refreshing later re-syncs these days and pulls in new conversations on them.
Pick the projects to bring in. Refreshin: Pick the projects to bring in. Refreshing later re-syncs these projects and pulls in their new conversations.
Your Claude Code and Codex conversations: Your Claude Code and Codex conversations as named agents — open one to keep chatting, edit what each imports, or refresh to pull in new conversations.
{{count}} conversations are no longer in: {{count}} conversations are no longer in scope. Remove them from your space?
Delete “{{name}}” and its {{count}} conv: Delete “{{name}}” and its {{count}} conversations? This removes them from your space and can't be undone.
